paper_title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
research_equation: Context Unawareness (LLMs cannot ground predictions in available evidence) + Contrastive Context Pairs (agent trajectories or images) + Context-Selection Loss as auxiliary RL objective → Improved context grounding across both agentic and multimodal settings, measurable via OOD transfer benchmarks

tri_pass_notes.what: LLMs frequently fail to use available context evidence when generating answers. Authors propose ContextRL, which adds an explicit context-selection auxiliary objective to standard GRPO post-training, training the model to distinguish between a supporting context and a minimally-different confounder given a query-answer pair.
tri_pass_notes.how: 1) Build contrastive context pairs via cascade filtering (agentic: 1k trajectory pairs from 66k) and generative editing + similarity retrieval (multimodal: 7k image pairs). 2) Define a logit-level contrastive loss ℒCA that widens the margin between positive and negative context logits, with clipping to prevent large margins from dominating. 3) Jointly optimize ℒ = ℒGRPO + λℒCA, where λ balances the two objectives. 4) The auxiliary signal is computed by teacher forcing (no rollout needed for ℒCA) and the GRPO clipping + KL regularization keep the policy stable.
tri_pass_notes.why: Standard outcome-only RL (GRPO) only rewards correct answers, providing no signal about whether the answer is grounded in the provided context. The context-awareness loss provides a dense, process-level signal that directly supervises context grounding without disrupting the policy distribution (unlike DA-SFT which causes catastrophic forgetting). The method is modality-agnostic and works the same way for agent trajectories and images.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Context unawareness: models produce correct-looking answers without grounding in available evidence
challenge_module_map.key_idea: 200+200 contrastive pairs with a two-choice selection task reveals a 40-point gap between proprietary and open-source models, quantifying the extent of the problem

challenge_module_map.challenge: Constructing high-quality contrastive context pairs that are hard enough to provide useful training signal
challenge_module_map.key_idea: Cascade filtering: same repo → same file → same function → related issues + patch masking + GPT-5.4 verification. Only 1.5% of 66k trajectories survive for agentic setting.

challenge_module_map.challenge: Constructing contrastive image pairs across diverse visual domains (charts, geometry, science, natural images)
challenge_module_map.key_idea: Two-pronged strategy: generative editing (Nano Banana 2, ~700 pairs from 2k, 65% rejection) for natural images + similarity retrieval (Qwen3-VL-Embedding, threshold α=0.85, 6,300 pairs from 200k+) for structured images

challenge_module_map.challenge: Integrating context-selection signal into RL without destabilizing the policy
challenge_module_map.key_idea: Teacher-forced logit contrast: no rollout needed; margin clipping (c=5.0) prevents large gradients from dominating; joint optimization with GRPO provides both answer-correctness and context-grounding signals

challenge_module_map.challenge: Verifying that gains come from the objective rather than from the data itself
challenge_module_map.key_idea: DA-SFT (collapses agentic policy, 0% resolve rate on Qwen3-8B) and DA-RL (no improvement over RL baseline) are compared against ContextRL, showing the objective is key
